name: Open-Meteo
description: Free weather forecast API — no API key, attribution required for commercial use. Returns current weather, hourly/daily forecasts, historical reanalysis. DACH-relevant: native support for any lat/lon, default units metric, timezone-aware. Pair with /v1/geocoding for city-name lookup.

auth.instructions: No API key required for the free tier (≤10k requests/day, non-commercial). Commercial use must purchase a key and use a separate base URL; for the free public endpoint the request is unauthenticated.

endpoints.description: Forecast for a lat/lon. Required: latitude, longitude. Common: hourly=temperature_2m,precipitation, daily=weather_code,temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min, timezone=auto.
Cheap current-conditions probe — single object, ~200 bytes.
Historical reanalysis (ERA5) from 1940 onwards. Same param shape as /forecast plus start_date + end_date.

guidelines: Always pass `timezone=auto` — without it the API returns UTC timestamps, which is rarely what the user wants.
Resolve city → lat/lon via the separate geocoding endpoint (`https://geocoding-api.open-meteo.com/v1/search?name=Zürich`) once, then cache the coordinates.
For a one-off 'what's the weather right now' query, prefer `current=` over `hourly=` — it returns ~200 bytes instead of 10–20 KB.
Include source attribution when surfacing results to end users: 'Weather data: Open-Meteo'.
avoid: Don't request `hourly=` + 14-day range when you only need today — that's ~40 KB of JSON for no reason.
Don't poll faster than once per ~15 minutes per lat/lon — the underlying model only updates hourly.
notes: Free tier is genuinely free for non-commercial + low-volume commercial use, but the terms require attribution and forbid resale.
DACH-friendly: handles ä/ö/ü/ß in geocoding queries; returns Celsius/km-h by default.
